free_text: Cross sections for neutrons of 1970 eV and contribu-
tions to fundamental neutron interactions.
The 4 MW research reactor FRM.
Reactor neutrons from a tangential beam tube
were scattered in a 0.25 mm metal sheet of 5 g of
Cu-63, enriched to 99.6%. The neutrons are
collimated and filtered in a combination of Sc, Co and
B4C stationary filters. The neutrons then pass reso-
nance and compensation filters before passing the
samples. A second scatterer of 1.28 g Se-80, enriched
to 99.2%, is placed close to the detectors.
1.97 keV neutrons with a resolution of 50 eV.
Filtered neutrons. Double scattering in Cu-63
and Se-80, the first having a resonance at 2038+/-3 eV
with a width of 62.7 eV, and the second with a
resonance at 1970+/-19 eV.
The detector consisted of four BF3 counters,
enriched to 90% in B-10, having a diameter of
5 cm and an effective length of 20 cm. They were
arranged parallel to and around the rectangular detec-
tor tube, which contained the Se-80 scatterer.
Some data were extrapolated to zero sample thickness.
No details given.
N.O.

following E.Dupont's comment.

obtained by extrapolation from higher energy region
Measured with a filtered beam using a 127 cm long
silicon filter in the reactor.
Data from tables 4 and 5 of ZP/A,337,341
SD: SF3:TOT -> THS, SF5=FA added in
REACTION code (2).
.Determined from experimental data at higher energy.
Local field refinements by sears.
